stem: An engineer takes 25 measurements of room temperature using a thermometer. The measurements have a mean of 106.9 °C and a standard deviation of 5.9 °C. However, calibration tests reveal that a thermometer systematically underestimates the true value by between 2.1% and 7.2%. Estimate the true room temperature and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 106.9 °C. After correcting for the 4.7% bias: ≈ 112.2 °C. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.1%-7.2%) and sampling variability: [106.8, 117.6]. Point estimate: 112.2 °C.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
